rationale: The cited paper ("To Bargain or Not to Bargain: The Role of Fixed Costs in Price Negotiations") directly studies consumers’ decision to initiate bargaining under hybrid pricing, which overlaps the citing paper’s core mechanism that ARP/IPD affects negotiation initiation and negotiated outcomes. The citing note explicitly says it "refines prior bargaining studies that assume consumers always negotiate," matching the cited paper’s stated contribution on fixed bargaining costs and bargain-or-not decisions; with an exact DOI match, this is plausibly a substantive prior-work link rather than an incidental mention.
